programs.name: Hoosier Care Connect
programs.what_it_does: Hoosier Care Connect is a Medicaid program providing health care coverage for seniors aged 65 and older, blind, or disabled individuals who are not eligible for Medicare. It offers medical benefits to low-income members meeting specific aid categories.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Indiana residents aged 65+, blind, or disabled, with low income, not eligible for Medicare.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Full health care coverage for eligible low-income seniors; specific value varies by needs and services approved.

programs.name: Indiana PathWays for Aging Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The PathWays for Aging Waiver provides home and community-based services (HCBS) as an alternative to nursing facility placement, allowing seniors to live independently at home or in community settings. Services include participant-directed home care, respite care, transportation, and specialized medical equipment, customized via a Plan of Care.[2][3][4]
programs.who_its_for: Indiana Medicaid enrollees aged 60+, blind or disabled, meeting nursing facility level of care, with income ≤300% SSI (~$2,829/month single in 2024) and assets ≤$2,000 single/$3,000 couple.[2][3]
programs.benefit_value: Nursing facility level of care at home; annual cost limit ~$120,000 per participant (statewide waiver cap).

programs.name: Indiana PathWays for Aging (PACE equivalent)
programs.what_it_does: PathWays for Aging incorporates PACE-like managed long-term services and supports for Medicaid enrollees, coordinating care to enable aging at home with nursing-level services in community settings. It partners with health plans for care coordination, including home health, meals, and transportation.[4][5][7]
programs.who_its_for: Hoosiers aged 60+ on Medicaid (including dually eligible), blind/disabled, or in nursing facilities/hospice.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive LTSS managed care; supports 120,000 Hoosiers with personalized services.

programs.name: Healthy Indiana Plan Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Indiana's Medicare Savings Programs (Qualified Medicare Beneficiary - QMB, Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary - SLMB, Qualifying Individual - QI) pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copays for eligible low-income seniors. They are administered through traditional Medicaid for dually eligible members.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries; income limits vary (e.g., QMB ~100% FPL, SLMB ~120%, QI ~135%).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Covers Part A/B premiums/costs; e.g., QMB pays full Part B premium (~$174.70/month).

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing food at approved retailers. In Indiana, it's administered by the Division of Family Resources for low-income households including seniors.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (60+) with income ≤130-165% FPL (net ~$1,580/month single); higher deductions for elderly.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household; max ~$291 single (2026 adjusted).

programs.name: Indiana LIHEAP
programs.what_it_does: The Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) helps with heating/cooling bills and crisis assistance. Administered statewide for energy-burdened low-income households.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (≤150% FPL, ~$22,590 single); priority for seniors 60+, disabled.[1]

programs.name: Indiana Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce utility costs. Federally funded, state-administered for low-income homes.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income (≤200% FPL) owner/renter households; priority seniors, disabled.[1]

programs.name: Indiana SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free counseling on Medicare, supplements, and savings programs. Local counselors assist with enrollment and appeals.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; saves avg $1,000+/year via proper enrollment.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (Indiana)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors, often with friendly visits. Administered via Area Agencies on Aging (AAAs).[1]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals; suggested donation.[1]

programs.name: Indiana Caregiver Respite Services
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for family caregivers via in-home, adult day, or facility respite. Available through PathWays Waiver and AAAs.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+ on Medicaid waivers.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Up to 240 hours/year structured respite; varies by plan.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service jobs with training for older low-income workers. Federally funded, state partners administer.[1]

programs.name: Indiana Senior Legal Aid
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for issues like benefits, housing, abuse via Legal Services organizations and AAAs. Pro bono and low-cost services.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free representation; resolves issues worth thousands.

programs.name: Indiana Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living; investigates complaints, ensures rights. Statewide volunteer network.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves 80% complaints without escalation.
